preview_text:
The model favors Ivory Coast to win at 64%, a commanding advantage over Curaçao's 12% win probability. The implied gap reflects a substantial mismatch in underlying strength between the two sides. Curaçao retains a narrow path to victory but enters as a heavy underdog by the model's assessment.

The match shape reveals meaningful competitive space. A draw carries 25% probability under the model, suggesting the fixture is not a foregone conclusion and carries genuine tactical uncertainty. Ivory Coast's advantage is clear, yet the width of the draw band indicates the teams could plausibly reach a stalemate, preventing either side from decisively controlling the contest from start to finish.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices align closely with each other but diverge notably from the model. Books and Polymarket both price Ivory Coast at 83%, versus the model's 64%—an 19-percentage-point gap. The market treats Curaçao (5%) and draws (12%) as substantially less likely than the model does. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is correct; the divergence warrants awareness when assessing true match probability.
